pattern: The count of nice divisors equals the product of the chosen prime multiplicities, so split the budget into parts maximizing their product, which favors threes with twos cleaning up the remainder.
hints: Using a single prime taken to a high power, the nice divisors are exactly that prime's powers, so distinct primes multiply their exponents together.
The task reduces to breaking the integer budget into positive parts whose product is largest.
Greedily use parts of size three; if the remainder is 1 trade one three for two twos, if the remainder is 2 keep a single two, all under the modulus.

edge_inputs.description: four needs two twos not a three plus one
five splits as three plus two
ten where remainder one forces the swap

stress.description: random budget small enough for the brute-force DP oracle to stay fast
